note: Reference-labeler non-conversion regression. Same gap shape as specimen-002 (third-party / no default rule), with is_reference_labeler=true on LabelObservation. The expected outcome is IDENTICAL to specimen-002 modulo subject. If a future classifier change treats is_reference_labeler as a conversion promoter, this packet's gap classification will diverge from the expected here and verify.py will fail. That is the regression guard.

expected_admissible_claims.claim: A label record with label_value='fundraising-link' exists on at://did:plc:s6ktgg7ckbyz6bs66dlag6dj/app.bsky.feed.post/3mntabl3vcs2i, issued by skywatch.blue at 2026-06-09T03:25:22.950309Z.
Under the named consumer's published policy pipeline at the pinned artifact, no rule exists that maps this label_value to any render action.
From this evidence bundle, no constraint may be derived for the named consumer from this label record.
expected_admissible_claims.qualifier: Epistemic non-derivation, not a factual assertion of non-occurrence. Scoped to the named consumer.

expected_inadmissible_claims.why_inadmissible: Laundering: collapses producer/consumer/enforcer distinction. label_observed alone does not derive render_observed. The no-laundering theorem forbids this derivation.

expected_inadmissible_claims.claim_form: This post was rendered with action X for user U at time T
expected_inadmissible_claims.why_inadmissible: Requires RenderObservation, which is absent. Cannot be derived from LabelObservation + PolicyDocumentation alone.

expected_inadmissible_claims.claim_form: All renders of this post produce the documented action
expected_inadmissible_claims.why_inadmissible: Population claim. Policy may be configurable; viewer may override; live client may run a different policy version. Requires probe campaign with defined sampling frame, not derivation from this evidence bundle.

expected_inadmissible_claims.claim_form: This label produced no constraint anywhere
expected_inadmissible_claims.why_inadmissible: Factual non-occurrence claim. The evidence bundle is silent over other consumer configurations; inferring a global negative from a scoped derivation is the laundering shape in reverse. Per the derivation frame: evidence bundles derive admissible claims, they do not assert factual non-occurrence.
Out of scope. Labelwatch observes; whether testimony is meaningful is a different judgment requiring different inputs. The calculus must remain silent on importance.

expected_inadmissible_claims.claim_form: The labeler's labeling activity has no operational effect
expected_inadmissible_claims.why_inadmissible: Adoption is a free variable. The evidence bundle documents one consumer's zero-conversion case; it does not measure the labeler's reach across all possible consumer configurations.

load_bearing_assertion.claim: The gap classification under is_reference_labeler=true is IDENTICAL to the gap classification under is_reference_labeler=false for the same evidence shape. Specifically: expected_gap = {name: conversion_witness_gap_no_consumer, surface: null, consumer_scope: emitter_declared} — same as specimen-002. If a future classifier change reads is_reference_labeler and promotes consumer_scope to global_platform (or otherwise alters gap shape), this specimen's verify.py result will diverge from the expected here. That divergence IS the regression guard.
anchored_in: LabelerEmitterDocumentation.consumer_scope='emitter_declared' (must not promote to global_platform per classifier line 131 docstring)
F-001 (reference-labeler status does not imply default-client conversion)
load_bearing_assertion.note: The classifier does not currently emit an explicit no_reference_or_popularity_promotion inadmissible. The doctrine is enforced through the consumer_scope ladder (emitter_declared remains emitter_declared regardless of is_reference_labeler). This 'load_bearing_assertion' field documents the doctrinal point that the gap-shape match is testing; verify.py asserts the gap match, this field captures why.
